title: Parallel Worker Dispatch
rules: Work only on the product files assigned in the packet payload.
Run product tests, builds, linters, and commits according to the worker prompt.
Do not mutate Cadre control-plane files, native Cadre event/message state, provider state, worktree
Return structured evidence to the coordinator: worker id, task id, repo,
If product implementation fails, return the failure evidence. The coordinator
Use only `cadre_parallel`, `cadre_complete_task`, and other Cadre MCP packets
Treat `parallel_state.json` and native Cadre event/message state as packet-owned artifacts.
A worker conflict, failed test, missing commit, low coverage, or missing native task evidence
Product shell commands are allowed only for product implementation,

sections.heading: Packet Flow
sections.body:
1. Call `cadre_workflow` with `workflow: "implement"` and the selected `trackId`
   to obtain implementation prep and the phase schedule.
2. For a parallel phase, call `cadre_parallel` with `action: "plan"` or
   `action: "next_wave"` to obtain the ready worker set.
3. Call `cadre_parallel` with `action: "setup_workers"` and the current client
   `agentIdentifier` (`"claude"`, `"codex"`, `"copilot"`, or `"antigravity"`) to get the packet-owned
   integration worktree, sibling worker worktree, worker ref, and selected dispatch adapter. Add `execute:true` only
   when the user or workflow has authorized mutation.
4. Dispatch exactly the workers returned by the packet. Do not add, remove,
   reorder, split, or merge workers outside the packet result.
5. As each worker finishes, call `cadre_parallel` with `action: "record_finish"`
   and the worker result evidence. Use `execute:true` when recording completion.
6. After the wave finishes, call `cadre_parallel` with `action: "merge_back"` for
   the packet-owned merge plan. Merge-back targets the repo integration worktree,
   not `repos/<repo>` or a worker worktree. Add `execute:true` only when ready to mutate.
7. Call `cadre_parallel` with `action: "cleanup"` for packet-owned cleanup. Add
   `execute:true` only after merge-back is clean or the packet explicitly marks a
   worker safe to clean.
8. Repeat `next_wave` until the packet reports no ready workers.

All dependency gates, file collision checks, repo routing, event/message updates,
parallel audit state, merge conflict state, task completion, and cleanup are
packet-owned.

sections.heading: Dispatching Each Worker
sections.body:
<!-- AGENT:claude -->
Pass `agentIdentifier:"claude"` to `cadre_parallel` when setting up workers.
Then call the **`Task` tool** once per packet worker using the MCP-returned
`selected_dispatch`, worker prompt, worktree, owned files, and expected result
schema. Worker calls are awaitable; wait for every worker in the wave before
calling the next Cadre packet.
<!-- /AGENT:claude -->
<!-- AGENT:codex -->
Pass `agentIdentifier:"codex"` to `cadre_parallel` when setting up workers.
Then use Codex multi-agent tools from the MCP-returned `selected_dispatch`.
Search tool discovery for `multi_agent_v1.spawn_agent`, spawn one worker agent
per packet worker, pass the worker prompt and dispatch payload, and wait with
the corresponding multi-agent wait tool before calling the next Cadre packet.
If the selected dispatch mechanism is unavailable, halt with a clear
`dispatch-unavailable` message instead of simulating worker state locally.
<!-- /AGENT:codex -->
<!-- AGENT:copilot -->
Pass `agentIdentifier:"copilot"` to `cadre_parallel` when setting up workers.
Invoke the installed Cadre worker custom agent for each packet worker, passing
the MCP-returned prompt, dispatch payload, worktree, owned files, and expected
result schema. Copilot `/fleet` may be used only when it preserves the Cadre
record_finish contract for each returned worker.
<!-- /AGENT:copilot -->
<!-- AGENT:antigravity -->
Pass `agentIdentifier:"antigravity"` to `cadre_parallel` when setting up workers.
Use `invoke_subagent`, or define and invoke a Cadre worker subagent, once per
packet worker with the MCP-returned prompt and dispatch payload. Wait for every
subagent before calling the next Cadre packet.
<!-- /AGENT:antigravity -->

sections.heading: Worker Rules
sections.body:
- Work only on the product files assigned in the packet payload.
- Run product tests, builds, linters, and commits according to the worker prompt.
- Use the worker worktree returned by the packet. Worker worktrees are siblings
  of integration worktrees and must not be created inside integration worktrees.
- Do not mutate Cadre control-plane files, native Cadre event/message state, provider state, worktree
  topology, merge state, or cleanup state directly.
- Return structured evidence to the coordinator: worker id, task id, repo,
  commit evidence, worker ref when supplied, tests run, coverage when available,
  files changed, summary, and blockers.
- If product implementation fails, return the failure evidence. The coordinator
  records it through Cadre packets.

sections.heading: Coordinator Rules
sections.body:
- Use only `cadre_parallel`, `cadre_complete_task`, and other Cadre MCP packets
  named by packet results for Cadre state changes.
- Treat `parallel_state.json` and native Cadre event/message state as packet-owned artifacts.
- A worker conflict, failed test, missing commit, low coverage, or missing native
  task evidence is handled by the packet result. Report the packet error and halt when
  it is blocking.
- Product shell commands are allowed only for product implementation,
  verification, and commits inside worker scope.

codex.dispatch: Pass agentIdentifier:"codex" to setup_workers. Use the returned selected_dispatch to discover multi_agent_v1.spawn_agent, spawn one worker per packet worker, wait for all workers, then call the next Cadre packet. If unavailable, halt with dispatch-unavailable.

claude.dispatch: Pass agentIdentifier:"claude" to setup_workers. Use the returned selected_dispatch to call the Task tool once per MCP-provided worker prompt. Pass dispatch payload, worktree, owned files, expected result schema, and wait for every worker before calling the next Cadre packet.

copilot.dispatch: Pass agentIdentifier:"copilot" to setup_workers. Invoke the installed Cadre worker custom agent once per packet worker, or use /fleet only when each worker still returns the Cadre record_finish contract.

antigravity.dispatch: Pass agentIdentifier:"antigravity" to setup_workers. Use invoke_subagent or a dynamically defined Cadre worker subagent once per packet worker, then wait for all subagents before the next Cadre packet.
